supersedes: New dated folder for 2026-07-20, the day after the final. Run 8 (match-in-progress) preserved in research/2026-07-19/, Runs 3-5 in research/2026-07-15/, Run 6 in research/2026-07-16/, Run 7 in research/2026-07-17/, all in git. This is the resolving pass: the tournament is over and every result-dependent open item from Run 8 is now graded.

redCards: Enzo Fernandez (ARG) 90+3' - second yellow (dissent, then reckless challenge on Cubarsi); ESPN/VAR review judged it deserved. ARG played extra time a man down.
Leandro Paredes (ARG) - straight red AFTER the final whistle, in the post-match brawl
final.officiatingNote: The decisive call (Enzo red) went AGAINST Argentina and VAR upheld it. The only errors flagged went against Spain: a disallowed Nico Williams goal (Merino foul on Otamendi; Hackett argued VAR should have restored it) and a Torres offside. Under maximum scrutiny, not one call favored Argentina - the cleanest possible disconfirmation of the favoritism thesis.
disallowedSpainGoals: Nico Williams (Merino foul on Otamendi in build-up)

records.spainNote: Second title (after 2010). First nation to hold the men's and women's World Cups simultaneously (women's 2023). First World Cup winner ever to concede only one goal across a tournament (8 matches), anchored by Cubarsi + Laporte; part of a 37-match unbeaten run (last defeat March 2024). Rodri captained.
records.messi: 39; left the pitch in tears with a runners-up medal in likely his last World Cup match. Second man after Cafu to APPEAR in three finals, and the FIRST to START all three (Cafu came on as a substitute in the 1994 final). This reconciles Run 8's 'first to start three finals' with the 'second to appear' framing - both true.

awards.goldenGlove: Unai Simon (ESP) - one goal conceded in 8 matches
awards.goldenBoot: Kylian Mbappe (FRA), 10 goals to Messi's 8 - second straight, first player since Muller (1970) with more than 8 at a single men's World Cup

aftermath.brawl: After the whistle Molina appeared to swing at Rodri; Paredes grabbed Eric Garcia by the throat and wrestled Gavi to the ground (straight red). Argentina's players declined to join Spain's trophy lift.
aftermath.fifaCase: FIFA reported to be investigating Argentina over the brawl - a SECOND FIFA disciplinary matter stacked on the still-open Malvinas-banner case, with zero refereeing involved.
aftermath.malvinasRuling: STILL NOT ISSUED as of Jul 20 - FIFA had announced only that its disciplinary bodies were assessing it. 2014 CHF 30,000 remains the benchmark. Pending.

modelFavoriteWon.note: Opta match-day 59.5% Spain, market ~59%; Spain won. After both semifinal favorites lost (France 57.7% to reach final, England 52.53%), the final favorite finally held.

extraTimeTail.note: Opta ~29% ET; Argentina's 40.5% title number sat well above its 26% regulation-win number because the knockout path ran through ET; Kalshi regulation-only had a 32% draw. The final went to extra time and was decided there (Torres 106'). The tail the split flagged is exactly where it was won.

noProArgentinaBiasInFinal.note: H4 regression test. The decisive call went against Argentina; the only errors went against Spain. Zero pro-Argentina officiating under maximum scrutiny.

finalPassedWithoutMajorControversy_liter.note: There WAS major controversy - a game-shaping red, two disallowed Spain goals, ex-refs blasting the crew, a post-match brawl - but none of it favored Argentina, so the bias thesis the claim was really testing failed cleanly.

faghaniNeutralRefereeTip.note: Carried from Run 6/8; FIFA appointed Vincic, a European. Closed.

predictions.note: Forecasts are now RESOLVED. models.ts pre-match numbers are intentionally left as the graded record (not overwritten to 1/0). Bracket.ts carries the actual result.

market_preMatch.note: Firmed to ~59% Spain into kickoff; settled to Spain.

H1_deliberate_favoritism.note: NARROWED. The tournament is fully observed and its cleanest test disconfirmed the thesis: in the final, under maximum scrutiny, the decisive red went AGAINST Argentina (VAR-upheld), the only errors went against Spain, and Argentina lost. rangeHigh 0.08 -> 0.06; strong againstPoint added; source nbc-officiating-red attached. Not zeroed - one match doesn't retroactively clean the earlier rounds and the penalty outlier persists, but the ceiling comes down.

H2_chargeable_conduct.note: No legal movement. Still preliminary; no charges; FBI declines comment. changeMind (subpoena/indictment/cooperating witness) NOT met. Domestic hearing still Aug 12.

H4_variance_perception.note: STRENGTHENED. Its changeMind was 'if the pattern regresses under semifinal and final scrutiny, that confirms it.' It regressed: the final produced no pro-Argentina call and the post-match flashpoint was an Argentine-instigated brawl, not a referee. rangeLow 0.30 -> 0.35; regression-test forPoint added; sources nbc-officiating-red + yahoo-final-brawl attached.

carriedForwardFromRun8: All Run 1-8 verified facts stand: the group/knockout run; Spain 2-0 France with the disputed Digne-on-Yamal penalty; England 1-2 Argentina (referee Elfath, no controversy of substance); the Vincic final appointment (Run 6's neutral-field/Faghani reasoning graded a miss); the record prediction-market scale (Kalshi ESP-ARG contract >$1.27B, Polymarket winner market >$4B cumulative, total World Cup trading >$25B, with the liquidity-is-not-accuracy caveat since these markets favored BOTH losing semifinalists); the Malvinas banner + FIFA Article 34.3 review (2014 CHF 30,000 precedent); confirmed final elevens (Spain unchanged, Argentina three changes, Alvarez over Lautaro, Yamal + Porro fit); ticket market ($6,411.25 cheapest, gone by lunchtime, last-minute $10k-$60k); the money thread (five named banks, TourProdEnter ~$260M / ~$57M unexplained, Miami Herald ~$90M, Tofoni ~3h videoconference as witness, the Jul 14 inward turn, three named prosecutors, no US charges); Tapia in the main box near Trump; author ranges ESP 55-60 / ARG 40-45.
newSinceRun8: THE RESULT: Spain 1-0 Argentina after extra time (Torres 106', assist Nico Williams). Spain are 2026 world champions - second title. Enzo Fernandez red 90+3' (VAR-upheld); Argentina played ET a man down and had no shot on target in regulation. Attendance 80,663. Torres player of the match. bracket.ts FINAL.result filled; stage -> champion.

THE FAVORITISM THESIS FAILED ITS CLEANEST TEST: the final's decisive call went AGAINST Argentina and the only errors flagged (two disallowed Spain goals) went against Spain. NBC: 'A World Cup known for officiating controversy ends with Argentina seeing red.' H1 narrowed to 2-6%; H4 strengthened to 35-55%. Source nbc-officiating-red.

FORECAST GRADING: model favorite (Opta 59.5%, market ~59%) WON - hit; extra-time tail (~29-32%) HIT, the final went to ET and was decided there - hit. models.ts header records both without overwriting the pre-match numbers.

AWARDS (all but one to Spain): Golden Ball Rodri (Messi Silver, Mbappe Bronze); Golden Glove Unai Simon (1 goal in 8); Best Young Player Cubarsi (not Yamal); Golden Boot Mbappe 10 goals to Messi's 8. Source athlon-final-awards.

SPAIN RECORDS: first nation to hold men's + women's World Cups at once; first champion ever to concede only one goal across a tournament (Cubarsi + Laporte); 37-match unbeaten run. Source npr-spain-champion.
MESSI CODA: 39, left in tears with a runners-up medal in likely his last World Cup match; second after Cafu to appear in three finals, first to start all three.

POST-MATCH BRAWL + SECOND FIFA CASE: after the whistle Molina appeared to swing at Rodri, Paredes grabbed Eric Garcia by the throat and wrestled Gavi down (straight red), Argentina's players skipped Spain's trophy lift; FIFA reported to be investigating. A second FIFA disciplinary matter with zero refereeing in it. Added timeline node final-brawl (order 20). Source yahoo-final-brawl.
MALVINAS RULING STILL NOT ISSUED as of Jul 20 (only 'under assessment'); 2014 CHF 30,000 remains the benchmark. Pending.
MONEY THREAD: no legal movement - no US charges, still preliminary, FBI declines comment. H2 changeMind unmet, bands held. Domestic hearing still Aug 12.
dataChangesThisRun: bracket.ts: FINAL.result filled (ESP 1-0 ARG, winner ESP, bilingual note). bracketStage() -> champion; argentinaEliminated() -> true. Header comment updated to final state.
models.ts: header gains a RESULT-IN grading note (favorite won, extra-time tail hit); pre-match probabilities intentionally left as the graded forecast.
bayes.ts: unchanged (config; the island back-solves priors from the published bands, so the H1/H4 band moves flow through automatically).
timeline.json: added final-result (order 19, sporting, Jul 19) and final-brawl (order 20, sporting, Jul 19).
hypotheses.json: H1 rangeHigh 0.08 -> 0.06 + againstPoint + source nbc-officiating-red; H4 rangeLow 0.30 -> 0.35 + forPoint + sources nbc-officiating-red, yahoo-final-brawl.
sources.json: added espn-final-report (105), npr-spain-champion (106), athlon-final-awards (107), nbc-officiating-red (108), yahoo-final-brawl (109), all tier 2.
sections.json: whowins-intro reconciled to the result in both locales (forecast kept, resolution appended: Spain won 1-0 AET, favorite + extra-time tail landed).
precedents.json NOT edited (no new fixing precedent).
.update-pending-log.json written: recommend true, Log run 7 (id run-7-2026-07-20).
openForNextRun: FIFA's Malvinas-banner ruling: warning, fine, or dismissal. 2014 CHF 30,000 is the benchmark to grade against.
The post-final brawl case: any FIFA sanction or hearing date.
The money thread's first formal step (subpoena / indictment / cooperating witness) that would move H2's secondary band; the Aug 12 domestic hearing; Tapia's travel-bond return.

Replace the paywalled Silver PELE per-team split with a printed number if Silver ever publishes one (sixth run unavailable).
